query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "beyond scalar rewards by internalizing reasoning into score distributi" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (b) as it investigates the same core mechanism (using follow-up reactions/likelihood as a reward signal for LLM alignment) and addresses the same dependent variable (improving model helpfulness via internalized reasoning/feedback) that the user's question references, even though it proposes a specific implementation (FLR) rather than just analyzing the prior work.
